speaker: ANTONY
text: These many, then, shall die; their names are pricked.

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: Your brother too must die. Consent you, Lepidus?

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: I do consent.

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: Prick him down, Antony.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Upon condition Publius shall not live,

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Who is your sister’s son, Mark Antony.

speaker: ANTONY
text: He shall not live; look, with a spot I damn him.

speaker: ANTONY
text: But, Lepidus, go you to Caesar’s house;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Fetch the will hither, and we shall determine

speaker: ANTONY
text: How to cut off some charge in legacies.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: What, shall I find you here?

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: Or here, or at the Capitol.

speaker: ANTONY
text: This is a slight, unmeritable man,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Meet to be sent on errands. Is it fit,

speaker: ANTONY
text: The threefold world divided, he should stand

speaker: ANTONY
text: One of the three to share it?

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: So you thought him

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: And took his voice who should be pricked to die

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: In our black sentence and proscription.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Octavius, I have seen more days than you,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And, though we lay these honors on this man

speaker: ANTONY
text: To ease ourselves of diverse sland’rous loads,

speaker: ANTONY
text: He shall but bear them as the ass bears gold,

speaker: ANTONY
text: To groan and sweat under the business,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Either led or driven, as we point the way;

speaker: ANTONY
text: And having brought our treasure where we will,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Then take we down his load and turn him off

speaker: ANTONY
text: (Like to the empty ass) to shake his ears

speaker: ANTONY
text: And graze in commons.

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: You may do your will,

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: But he’s a tried and valiant soldier.

speaker: ANTONY
text: So is my horse, Octavius, and for that

speaker: ANTONY
text: I do appoint him store of provender.

speaker: ANTONY
text: It is a creature that I teach to fight,

speaker: ANTONY
text: To wind, to stop, to run directly on,

speaker: ANTONY
text: His corporal motion governed by my spirit;

speaker: ANTONY
text: And, in some taste, is Lepidus but so.

speaker: ANTONY
text: He must be taught and trained and bid go forth—

speaker: ANTONY
text: A barren-spirited fellow, one that feeds

speaker: ANTONY
text: On objects, arts, and imitations

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which, out of use and staled by other men,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Begin his fashion. Do not talk of him

speaker: ANTONY
text: But as a property. And now, Octavius,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Listen great things. Brutus and Cassius

speaker: ANTONY
text: Are levying powers. We must straight make head.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Therefore let our alliance be combined,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Our best friends made, our means stretched;

speaker: ANTONY
text: And let us presently go sit in council

speaker: ANTONY
text: How covert matters may be best disclosed

speaker: ANTONY
text: And open perils surest answerèd.

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: Let us do so, for we are at the stake

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: And bayed about with many enemies,

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: And some that smile have in their hearts, I fear,

speaker: OCTAVIUS
text: Millions of mischiefs.
